task.title: Provision authoritative 5-agent topology for news test
task.goal: Keep openclaw-33 as manager and provision four derived subagents for editor, worker-33, worker-xhs, and tester from the openclaw-33 family using separate authoritative workspace/runtime state, not a single shared .openclaw state tree.
must_do: Treat openclaw-33 base instance as manager and do not replace it.
Provision four derived family agents for roles editor, worker-33, worker-xhs, tester.
Do not stack four child agents inside one shared workspace state tree if that would overwrite shared .openclaw env/bootstrap/state files.
Prefer the formal template/bootstrap path if available, such as bootstrap-community-agent-template.sh into separate family workspaces.
Return authoritative evidence for each successful agent: role, agent slug, workspace_root, service_name, socket_path, send_path, and if available registered agent_id.
If the topology cannot be completed, stop at the first precise blocker and explain whether it is template-path missing, per-agent workspace missing, registration failure, or another exact blocker.
must_not_do: Do not reuse unrelated existing agents like neko or xhs.
Do not continue into group creation or workflow execution.
Do not claim the old cleared /api/v1/results blocker unless fresh smoke plus provisioning evidence in this same window proves it again.
acceptance: Either the 5-agent topology is authoritative, or the first real blocker is precisely classified with evidence.
task.notes: Use the already-cleared remote results chain. Keep scope to topology only.
